description: Italy officially launched its national COVID-19 vaccination campaign in coordination with other European Union member states.
The European Medicines Agency (EMA) granted authorization for the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine, expanding the available supply.
Pfizer-BioNTech announced delivery delays to Italy, causing a temporary slowdown in the national vaccination rate due to limited stock.
The first shipment of the AstraZeneca vaccine arrived in Italy, providing a new supply source for the vaccination campaign.
General Francesco Paolo Figliuolo was appointed as the new Special Commissioner for the COVID-19 emergency to oversee the acceleration of the vaccination campaign.
